    <h1 {...{
      "id": "data-repair-api-faq"
    }}>{`Data Repair API FAQ`}</h1>
    <p>{`Frequently asked questions for the Data Repair API.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "if-i-accidentally-delete-incorrect-data-is-there-a-way-to-get-it-back"
    }}>{`If I accidentally delete incorrect data, is there a way to get it back?`}</h2>
    <p>{`No. `}<strong parentName="p">{`Data removed by the Data Repair API is permanently deleted and not retrievable.`}</strong>{` Make sure that your Data Repair API calls are correct before running them for large date ranges.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "how-long-does-a-data-repair-job-take"
    }}>{`How long does a data repair job take?`}</h2>
    <p>{`The amount of time a data repair job takes depends on the number of days processed and how much traffic a report suite gets per day. Jobs typically complete in hours but can take multiple days for large report suites with extended date ranges.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "what-happens-when-i-access-reports-in-adobe-analytics-while-a-data-repair-job-is-running"
    }}>{`What happens when I access reports in Adobe Analytics while a data repair job is running?`}</h2>
    <p>{`If you run a report in Adobe Analytics referencing a dimension and date range processed by the Data Repair API, it can return `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Unspecified`}</inlineCode>{`. Once the data repair job is complete, the values returned by Adobe Analytics (and not removed by the Data Repair API process) display normally.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "how-does-expiration-and-allocation-work-when-repairing-data"
    }}>{`How does expiration and allocation work when repairing data?`}</h2>
    <p>{`An eVar value can exist across multiple hits or visits depending on the expiration of the eVar. Consequently, when repairing an eVar, it is important that you check the expiration setting (and potentially use the "Reset" option for that eVar) to avoid historical data "re-populating" the variable. See `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://docs.adobe.com/content/help/en/analytics/admin/admin-tools/conversion-variables/conversion-var-admin.html"
      }}>{`Conversion Variables`}</a>{` in the Adobe Analytics Admin user guide for more information on eVar expiration and resetting persisted values.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "how-do-late-arriving-hits-work-when-repairing-data"
    }}>{`How do late arriving hits work when repairing data?`}</h2>
    <p>{`If `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://experienceleague.adobe.com/en/docs/analytics/export/analytics-data-feed/data-feed-contents/late-arriving-hits"
      }}>{`late arriving hits`}</a>{` are passed in after a repair has run for the time period covered by the repair, previously repaired data can be reintroduced:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Repairs that edit or remove values within hits:`}</strong>{` Late arriving hits can reintroduce the undesired values that the repair removed.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Repairs that delete entire hits:`}</strong>{` Late arriving hits that match the repair criteria are admitted into the report suite, even though they would have been deleted had they existed when the repair job ran.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`There is no way to selectively prevent late arriving hits from being processed. If the reintroduced data is unacceptable, you can run another data repair covering the affected time period once you are confident that there are no more late arriving hits for the desired date range.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "do-i-need-to-enable-a-disabled-variable-before-repairing-it"
    }}>{`Do I need to enable a disabled variable before repairing it?`}</h2>
    <p>{`Data Repair works on enabled or disabled variables. You do not need to enable a variable in order for Data Repair to act on it.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "why-arent-my-url-repairs-updating-data-as-expected"
    }}>{`Why aren't my URL repairs updating data as expected?`}</h2>
    <p>{`When applying a URL operator (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`deleteQueryString`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`deleteQueryStringParameters`}</inlineCode>{`), the Data Repair API makes sure that the value is a correctly formatted URL before taking any action. A common mistake for incorrectly formatted URLs is having unencoded characters, such as a space, in the URL.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "what-are-some-limitations-of-using-this-tool"
    }}>{`What are some limitations of using this tool?`}</h2>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Data repair job date ranges cannot include the current month. A job must start within the last 60 months and end prior to the current month.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Report suites with `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://experienceleague.adobe.com/docs/analytics/components/cda/overview.html"
        }}>{`Cross-Device Analytics`}</a>{` are not supported.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Any actions involving the editing or deletion of `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://experienceleague.adobe.com/docs/analytics/components/dimensions/entry-dimensions.html"
        }}>{`Metrics`}</a>{` are not supported.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`The `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://experienceleague.adobe.com/docs/analytics/components/dimensions/product.html"
        }}>{`Product`}</a>{` dimension is not supported.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Dimensions that currently have `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://experienceleague.adobe.com/docs/analytics/components/dimensions/evar-merchandising.html"
        }}>{`Merchandising`}</a>{` enabled (or had it enabled historically) are not supported. If you run a data repair job on a variable that has had merchandising enabled in the past, you risk seeing incorrect or unexpected attribution results.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Only one data repair job at a time can run per report suite.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "how-do-i-refresh-my-authentication-token"

      }}>{`Authentication Guide`}</a>{`.`}</p>

    </MDXLayout>;
